briefdescription.para: Node identifier for an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible.

simplesect.para: a node identifier for the node with DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible and instance number inst

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT parent's node identifier.

simplesect.para: a node identifier for the instance's parent

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT grandparent's node identifier.

simplesect.para: a node identifier for the instance's grandparent

briefdescription.para: Get a node identifier for a child node of DT_DRV_INST(inst)

simplesect.para: node identifier for the node with the name referred to by 'child'

briefdescription.para: Get a node identifier for a child node with a matching unit address of DT_DRV_INST(inst)

simplesect.para: Only works for children with unique integer unit addresses.

parameterdescription.para: Integer unit address for the child node.

simplesect.para: node identifier for the child node with the specified unit address

briefdescription.para: Get the number of child nodes of a given node.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to
DT_CHILD_NUM(DT_DRV_INST(inst)).
inst
Devicetree instance number
Number of child nodes

briefdescription.para: Get the number of child nodes of a given node.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to
DT_CHILD_NUM_STATUS_OKAY(DT_DRV_INST(inst)).
inst
Devicetree instance number
Number of child nodes which status are okay

briefdescription.para: Get a string array of DT_DRV_INST(inst)'s node labels.

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to DT_NODELABEL_STRING_ARRAY(DT_DRV_INST(inst)).

simplesect.para: an array initializer for an array of the instance's node labels as strings

briefdescription.para: Get the number of node labels by instance number.

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to DT_NUM_NODELABELS(DT_DRV_INST(inst)).

simplesect.para: the number of node labels that the node with that instance number has

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all child nodes of DT_DRV_INST(inst).

detaileddescription.para: The macro fn should take one argument, which is the node identifier for the child node.
The children will be iterated over in the same order as they appear in the final devicetree.

parameterdescription.para: macro to invoke on each child node identifier

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all child nodes of DT_DRV_INST(inst) with a separator.

detaileddescription.para: The macro fn should take one argument, which is the node identifier for the child node.

parameterdescription.para: macro to invoke on each child node identifier
Separator (e.g. comma or semicolon). Must be in parentheses; this is required to enable providing a comma as separator.

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all child nodes of DT_DRV_INST(inst).

detaileddescription.para: The macro fn takes multiple arguments. The first should be the node identifier for the child node. The remaining are passed-in by the caller.
The children will be iterated over in the same order as they appear in the final devicetree.

parameterdescription.para: macro to invoke on each child node identifier
variable number of arguments to pass to fn

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all child nodes of DT_DRV_INST(inst) with separator.

detaileddescription.para: The macro fn takes multiple arguments. The first should be the node identifier for the child node. The remaining are passed-in by the caller.

parameterdescription.para: macro to invoke on each child node identifier
Separator (e.g. comma or semicolon). Must be in parentheses; this is required to enable providing a comma as separator.
variable number of arguments to pass to fn

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all child nodes of DT_DRV_INST(inst) with status okay.

detaileddescription.para: The macro fn should take one argument, which is the node identifier for the child node.

parameterdescription.para: macro to invoke on each child node identifier

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all child nodes of DT_DRV_INST(inst) with status okay and with separator.

detaileddescription.para: The macro fn should take one argument, which is the node identifier for the child node.

parameterdescription.para: macro to invoke on each child node identifier
Separator (e.g. comma or semicolon). Must be in parentheses; this is required to enable providing a comma as separator.

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all child nodes of DT_DRV_INST(inst) with status okay and multiple arguments.

detaileddescription.para: The macro fn takes multiple arguments. The first should be the node identifier for the child node. The remaining are passed-in by the caller.

parameterdescription.para: macro to invoke on each child node identifier
variable number of arguments to pass to fn

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all child nodes of DT_DRV_INST(inst) with status okay and with separator and multiple arguments.

detaileddescription.para: The macro fn takes multiple arguments. The first should be the node identifier for the child node. The remaining are passed-in by the caller.

parameterdescription.para: macro to invoke on each child node identifier
Separator (e.g. comma or semicolon). Must be in parentheses; this is required to enable providing a comma as separator.
variable number of arguments to pass to fn

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all node reg property for a given DT_DRV_COMPAT instance.

detaileddescription.para: The macro fn takes two parameters, node_id which will be the node identifier of the node with the reg property and idx the index of the reg array.
Equivalent to DT_FOREACH_REG(DT_DRV_INST(inst), fn).

parameterdescription.para: macro to invoke on each reg property

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all node reg property for a given DT_DRV_COMPAT instance with separator.

detaileddescription.para: The macro fn takes two parameters, node_id which will be the node identifier of the node with the reg property and idx the index of the reg array.
Equivalent to DT_FOREACH_REG_SEP(DT_DRV_INST(inst), fn, sep).

parameterdescription.para: macro to invoke on each reg property
Separator (e.g. comma or semicolon). Must be in parentheses; this is required to enable providing a comma as separator.

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all node reg property for a given DT_DRV_COMPAT instance with multiple arguments.

detaileddescription.para: The macro fn takes multiple arguments. The first one node_id will be the node identifier of the node with the reg property and idx the index of the reg array. The remaining are passed-in by the caller.
Equivalent to DT_FOREACH_REG_VARGS(DT_DRV_INST(inst), fn, VA_ARGS).

parameterdescription.para: macro to invoke on each reg property
variable number of arguments to pass to fn

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all node reg property for a given DT_DRV_COMPAT instance with separator and multiple arguments.

detaileddescription.para: The macro fn takes multiple arguments. The first one node_id will be the node identifier of the node with the reg property and idx the index of the reg array. The remaining are passed-in by the caller.
Equivalent to DT_FOREACH_REG_SEP_VARGS(DT_DRV_INST(inst), fn, sep, VA_ARGS).

parameterdescription.para: macro to invoke on each reg property
Separator (e.g. comma or semicolon). Must be in parentheses; this is required to enable providing a comma as separator.
variable number of arguments to pass to fn

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT property array value's index into its enumeration values.

simplesect.para: zero-based index of the property's value in its enum: list

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT value's index into its enumeration values.

simplesect.para: zero-based index of the property's value in its enum: list

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_ENUM_IDX_BY_IDX(), but with a fallback to a default enum index.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback index value to expand to

simplesect.para: zero-based index of the property's value in its enum if present, default_idx_value otherwise

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_ENUM_IDX(), but with a fallback to a default enum index.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback index value to expand to

simplesect.para: zero-based index of the property's value in its enum if present, default_idx_value otherwise

briefdescription.para: Does a DT_DRV_COMPAT enumeration property have a given value by index?

simplesect.para: zero-based index of the property's value in its enum

briefdescription.para: Does a DT_DRV_COMPAT enumeration property have a given value?

simplesect.para: 1 if the node property has the value value, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance property.

simplesect.para: a representation of the property's value

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT property length.

simplesect.para: logical length of the property

briefdescription.para: Is index idx valid for an array type property on a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance?

simplesect.para: 1 if idx is a valid index into the given property, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Is name name available in a foo-names property?

simplesect.para: An expression which evaluates to 1 if name is an available name into the given property, and 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT element value in an array property.

simplesect.para: a representation of the idx-th element of the property

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_PROP(), but with a fallback to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: DT_INST_PROP(inst, prop) or default_value

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_PROP_LEN(), but with a fallback to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: DT_INST_PROP_LEN(inst, prop) or default_value

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's string property's value as a token.

simplesect.para: the value of prop as a token, i.e. without any quotes and with special characters converted to underscores

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_STRING_TOKEN(), but uppercased.

simplesect.para: the value of prop as an uppercased token, i.e. without any quotes and with special characters converted to underscores

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's string property's value as an unquoted sequence of tokens.

simplesect.para: the value of prop as a sequence of tokens, with no quotes

briefdescription.para: Get an element out of string-array property as a token.

simplesect.para: the element in prop at index idx as a token

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_STRING_TOKEN_BY_IDX(), but with a fallback to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the element in prop at index idx as a token, or default_value

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_STRING_TOKEN_BY_IDX(), but uppercased.

simplesect.para: the element in prop at index idx as an uppercased token

briefdescription.para: Get an element out of string-array property as an unquoted sequence of tokens.

simplesect.para: the value of prop at index idx as a sequence of tokens, with no quotes

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's property value from a phandle's node.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores property of inst with type phandle
lowercase-and-underscores property of the phandle's node

simplesect.para: the value of prop as described in the DT_PROP() documentation

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's property value from a phandle in a property.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores property with type phandle, phandles, or phandle-array
logical index into "phs", which must be zero if "phs" has type phandle
lowercase-and-underscores property of the phandle's node

simplesect.para: the value of prop as described in the DT_PROP() documentation

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's phandle-array specifier value at an index.

parameterdescription.para: logical index into the property pha
binding's cell name within the specifier at index idx

simplesect.para: the value of the cell inside the specifier at index idx

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_PHA_BY_IDX(), but with a fallback to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: logical index into the property pha
binding's cell name within the specifier at index idx
a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: DT_INST_PHA_BY_IDX(inst, pha, idx, cell) or default_value

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's phandle-array specifier value Equivalent to DT_INST_PHA_BY_IDX(inst, pha, 0, cell)

parameterdescription.para: binding's cell name for the specifier at pha index 0

simplesect.para: the cell value

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_PHA(), but with a fallback to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: binding's cell name for the specifier at pha index 0
a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: DT_INST_PHA(inst, pha, cell) or default_value

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's value within a phandle-array specifier by name.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores name of a specifier in pha
binding's cell name for the named specifier

simplesect.para: the cell value

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_PHA_BY_NAME(), but with a fallback to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores name of a specifier in pha
binding's cell name for the named specifier
a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: DT_INST_PHA_BY_NAME(inst, pha, name, cell) or default_value

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's phandle node identifier from a phandle array by name.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores name of an element in pha

simplesect.para: node identifier for the phandle at the element named "name"

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's node identifier for a phandle in a property.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores property name in inst with type phandle, phandles or phandle-array

simplesect.para: a node identifier for the phandle at index idx in prop

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's node identifier for a phandle property's value.

parameterdescription.para: lowercase-and-underscores property of inst with type phandle

simplesect.para: a node identifier for the node pointed to by "ph"

briefdescription.para: is idx a valid register block index on a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance?

simplesect.para: 1 if idx is a valid register block index, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: is name a valid register block name on a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance?

simplesect.para: 1 if name is a valid register block name, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's idx-th register block's raw address.

parameterdescription.para: index of the register whose address to return

simplesect.para: address of the instance's idx-th register block

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's idx-th register block's address.

parameterdescription.para: index of the register whose address to return

simplesect.para: address of the instance's idx-th register block

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance's idx-th register block's size.

parameterdescription.para: index of the register whose size to return

simplesect.para: size of the instance's idx-th register block

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT's register block address by name.

simplesect.para: address of the register block with the given name

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_REG_ADDR_BY_NAME(), but with a fallback to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: address of the register block specified by name if present, default_value otherwise

briefdescription.para: 64-bit version of DT_INST_REG_ADDR_BY_NAME()

detaileddescription.para: This macro version adds the appropriate suffix for 64-bit unsigned integer literals. Note that this macro is equivalent to DT_INST_REG_ADDR_BY_NAME() in linker/ASM context.

simplesect.para: address of the register block with the given name

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT's register block size by name.

simplesect.para: size of the register block with the given name

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_REG_SIZE_BY_NAME(), but with a fallback to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: size of the register block specified by name if present, default_value otherwise

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT's (only) register block raw address.

simplesect.para: instance's register block address

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT's (only) register block address.

simplesect.para: instance's register block address

briefdescription.para: 64-bit version of DT_INST_REG_ADDR()

detaileddescription.para: This macro version adds the appropriate suffix for 64-bit unsigned integer literals. Note that this macro is equivalent to DT_INST_REG_ADDR() in linker/ASM context.

simplesect.para: instance's register block address

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT's (only) register block size.

simplesect.para: instance's register block size

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT's number of interrupts.

simplesect.para: number of interrupts

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT interrupt level.

simplesect.para: interrupt level

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT interrupt specifier value at an index.

parameterdescription.para: logical index into the interrupt specifier array

simplesect.para: the named value at the specifier given by the index

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT interrupt specifier's interrupt controller by index.

simplesect.para: node_id of interrupt specifier's interrupt controller

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT interrupt specifier's interrupt controller by name.

simplesect.para: node_id of interrupt specifier's interrupt controller

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT interrupt specifier's interrupt controller.

simplesect.para: Equivalent to DT_INST_IRQ_INTC_BY_IDX(node_id, 0)
node_id of interrupt specifier's interrupt controller

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT interrupt specifier value by name.

simplesect.para: the named value at the specifier given by the index

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT interrupt specifier's value.

simplesect.para: the named value at that index

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT's (only) irq number.

simplesect.para: the interrupt number for the node's only interrupt

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT's irq number at index.

parameterdescription.para: logical index into the interrupt specifier array

simplesect.para: the interrupt number for the node's idx-th interrupt

briefdescription.para: Get a DT_DRV_COMPAT's bus node identifier.

simplesect.para: node identifier for the instance's bus node

briefdescription.para: Test if a DT_DRV_COMPAT's bus type is a given type.

parameterdescription.para: a binding's bus type as a C token, lowercased and without quotes

simplesect.para: 1 if the given instance is on a bus of the given type, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_STRING_TOKEN(), but with a fallback to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: if prop exists, its value as a token, i.e. without any quotes and with special characters converted to underscores. Otherwise default_value

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_STRING_UPPER_TOKEN(), but with a fallback to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the property's value as an uppercased token, or default_value

briefdescription.para: Like DT_INST_STRING_UNQUOTED(), but with a fallback to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: a fallback value to expand to

simplesect.para: the property's value as a sequence of tokens, with no quotes, or default_value

briefdescription.para: Test if any enabled node with the given compatible is on the given bus type.

detaileddescription.para: This is like DT_ANY_INST_ON_BUS_STATUS_OKAY(), except it can also be useful for handling multiple compatibles in single source file.
Example devicetree overlay:
Example usage, assuming i2c0 is an I2C bus controller node, and therefore temp is on an I2C bus:

parameterdescription.para: a binding's bus type as a C token, lowercased and without quotes

simplesect.para: 1 if any enabled node with that compatible is on that bus type, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Test if any DT_DRV_COMPAT node is on a bus of a given type and has status okay.

detaileddescription.para: This is a special-purpose macro which can be useful when writing drivers for devices which can appear on multiple buses. One example is a sensor device which may be wired on an I2C or SPI bus.
Example devicetree overlay:
Example usage, assuming i2c0 is an I2C bus controller node, and therefore temp is on an I2C bus:

parameterdescription.para: a binding's bus type as a C token, lowercased and without quotes

simplesect.para: 1 if any enabled node with that compatible is on that bus type, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Check if any DT_DRV_COMPAT node with status okay has a given property.

detaileddescription.para:
prop
lowercase-and-underscores property name
Example devicetree overlay:
Example usage:

briefdescription.para: Check if all DT_DRV_COMPAT node with status okay has a given property.

detaileddescription.para: If all nodes are disabled, this will return 1.

prop
lowercase-and-underscores property name
Example devicetree overlay:
Example usage:

briefdescription.para: Check if any device node with status okay has a given property.

detaileddescription.para:
compat
lowercase-and-underscores devicetree compatible
prop
lowercase-and-underscores property name
Example devicetree overlay:
Example usage:

briefdescription.para: Check if any DT_DRV_COMPAT node with status okay has a given boolean property that exists.

detaileddescription.para: This differs from DT_ANY_INST_HAS_PROP_STATUS_OKAY because even when not present on a node, the boolean property is generated with a value of 0 and therefore exists.

prop
lowercase-and-underscores property name
Example devicetree overlay:
Example usage:

briefdescription.para: Check if all DT_DRV_COMPAT node with status okay has a given boolean property that exists.

detaileddescription.para:
If all nodes are disabled, this will return 1.
prop
lowercase-and-underscores property name
Example devicetree overlay:
Example usage:

briefdescription.para: Check if any DT_DRV_COMPAT node with status okay has a given register name.
Check if all DT_DRV_COMPAT node with status okay has a given register name.

detaileddescription.para: If all nodes are disabled, this will return 1.

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all nodes with compatible DT_DRV_COMPAT and status okay

detaileddescription.para: This macro calls fn(inst) on each inst number that refers to a node with status okay. Whitespace is added between invocations.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example usage:
This expands to:
and from there, to either this: "DEV_A", "DEV_B", or this: "DEV_B", "DEV_A", No guarantees are made about the order that a and b appear in the expansion.
Note that fn is responsible for adding commas, semicolons, or other separators or terminators.
Device drivers should use this macro whenever possible to instantiate a struct device for each enabled node in the devicetree of the driver's compatible DT_DRV_COMPAT.

parameterdescription.para: Macro to call for each enabled node. Must accept an instance number as its only parameter.

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all nodes with compatible DT_DRV_COMPAT and status okay with multiple arguments.

parameterdescription.para: Macro to call for each enabled node. Must accept an instance number.
variable number of arguments to pass to fn

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all node labels for a given DT_DRV_COMPAT instance.

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to DT_FOREACH_NODELABEL(DT_DRV_INST(inst), fn).

parameterdescription.para: macro which will be passed each node label for the node with that instance number

briefdescription.para: Call fn on all node labels for a given DT_DRV_COMPAT instance with multiple arguments.

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to DT_FOREACH_NODELABEL_VARGS(DT_DRV_INST(inst), fn, ...).

parameterdescription.para: macro which will be passed each node label for the node with that instance number
additional arguments to pass to fn

briefdescription.para: Invokes fn for each element of property prop for a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance.

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to DT_FOREACH_PROP_ELEM(DT_DRV_INST(inst), prop, fn).

briefdescription.para: Invokes fn for each element of property prop for a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance with a separator.

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to DT_FOREACH_PROP_ELEM_SEP(DT_DRV_INST(inst), prop, fn, sep).

parameterdescription.para: Separator (e.g. comma or semicolon). Must be in parentheses; this is required to enable providing a comma as separator.

briefdescription.para: Invokes fn for each element of property prop for a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance with multiple arguments.

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to DT_FOREACH_PROP_ELEM_VARGS(DT_DRV_INST(inst), prop, fn, VA_ARGS)

parameterdescription.para: variable number of arguments to pass to fn

briefdescription.para: Invokes fn for each element of property prop for a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance with multiple arguments and a separator.

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to DT_FOREACH_PROP_ELEM_SEP_VARGS(DT_DRV_INST(inst), prop, fn, sep, VA_ARGS)

parameterdescription.para: Separator (e.g. comma or semicolon). Must be in parentheses; this is required to enable providing a comma as separator.
variable number of arguments to pass to fn

briefdescription.para: Does a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance have a property?

simplesect.para: 1 if the instance has the property, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Does a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance have the compatible?

simplesect.para: 1 if the instance matches the compatible, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Does a phandle array have a named cell specifier at an index for a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance?

parameterdescription.para: named cell value whose existence to check

simplesect.para: 1 if the named cell exists in the specifier at index idx, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Does a phandle array have a named cell specifier at index 0 for a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance?

parameterdescription.para: named cell value whose existence to check

simplesect.para: 1 if the named cell exists in the specifier at index 0, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: is index valid for interrupt property on a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance?

parameterdescription.para: logical index into the interrupt specifier array

simplesect.para: 1 if the idx is valid for the interrupt property 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Does a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance have an interrupt named cell specifier?

parameterdescription.para: named cell value whose existence to check

simplesect.para: 1 if the named cell exists in the interrupt specifier at index idx 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Does a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance have an interrupt value?

parameterdescription.para: named cell value whose existence to check

simplesect.para: 1 if the named cell exists in the interrupt specifier at index 0 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Does a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance have an interrupt value?

simplesect.para: 1 if name is a valid named specifier
